name: FaceSwapAI Controlled Group Photo Face-Size Detection Study
purpose: A controlled observation of how many faces the FaceSwapAI production detector returns when the same four-person group photo is resized from 1200 to 32 pixels wide.

design.controlledVariable: Native image width. Every sample uses the same uncropped group photo and aspect ratio.
design.resize: sharp 0.34.5 Lanczos3 resize; JPEG quality 90; 4:4:4 chroma subsampling.

measurement.detectorContract: Face count and bounding boxes only; no face crops, embeddings, identity matching or generation output.
measurement.detectionRate: Returned face count divided by four visually present subjects.
measurement.sequence: Samples were measured independently from largest to smallest native width.

limitations: This is one staged four-person photo and one detector snapshot, not a population benchmark or universal minimum-face-size threshold.
Downscaling changes both native image dimensions and recoverable facial detail; the study does not isolate every possible cause of a missed face.
A returned box confirms detection only. It does not measure identity preservation, blend quality, realism or generation success.
The observed order in which subjects disappear is specific to this composition, pose, lighting, accessories and detector version.
No private user image or generated face-swap output is included.

samples.label: 1200 pixels wide
samples.observation: Control resize. All four faces are returned; the smallest returned box is 138 pixels wide.

samples.label: 480 pixels wide
samples.observation: All four faces remain detectable; the smallest returned box is 55 pixels wide.

samples.label: 240 pixels wide
samples.observation: All four faces remain detectable; the smallest returned box is 27 pixels wide.

samples.label: 120 pixels wide
samples.observation: All four faces are still returned, including a box only 15 pixels wide.

samples.label: 96 pixels wide
samples.observation: The smallest subject in the control is no longer returned; three boxes remain.

samples.label: 80 pixels wide
samples.observation: Two faces are returned and two are missed; the remaining boxes are 11 and 13 pixels wide.

samples.label: 48 pixels wide
samples.observation: Only the rightmost face is returned, with a seven-pixel-wide box.

samples.label: 32 pixels wide
samples.observation: No face bounding box is returned.
